edges.note: Edmondson extends Argyris's work on defensive routines into psychological safety
Co-developed reflective practice and theory of action
Both address structure-agency; different mechanisms (habitus vs structuration)
Double bind theory directly informs Argyris's work on defensive routines
Both study interaction patterns; Goffman operationalises micro-mechanisms of the double bind
Both critique institutional education; Freire from pedagogy, Illich from institutional analysis
Drucker responds to and transcends Taylor's separation of thinking from doing
Deming's systems view is a direct response to Taylor's reductionism
Illich's institutional critique extends Weber's iron cage to education and learning
March's exploitation/exploration extends Weber's analysis of bureaucratic rationality
Westrum extends Deming's systems thinking to organisational information flow
Both study resilience; Seligman diagnoses helplessness, Bandura prescribes mastery experience
Dweck extends Seligman's explanatory style into growth/fixed mindset distinction
- Heifetz's adaptive leadership builds on Follett's integration concept - test 2 -
Edmondson's safety concept operationalises conditions for bypassing Goffman's face-work
Nonaka extends Drucker's knowledge worker concept with the SECI model
Both inform understanding of resistance; Kahneman explains loss aversion, Weick prescribes small wins
Both critique institutional control; Stacey from complexity, Illich from social criticism
Core concept: durable dispositions acquired through lived experience
Forms of capital determine position in the field
The lag between changed field and unchanged habitus
Contradictory messages at different logical levels
Lessons taught by the institutional form itself
Monopoly over the conditions of the activity
When the cure causes the disease at clinical, social, cultural levels
Convivial vs manipulative institutions
Managed performance vs dropped performance
Mutual face protection maintaining double binds
- Transformation creates stigma; communicated through expressions given off - test -
Routines provide existential continuity
Signification, domination, legitimation must move together
Losses loom twice as large as gains
Organisational patterns preventing double-loop learning
What people say vs what they do
Undiscussables are frames, not hidden facts
The floor for learning
Distinguishing technical from adaptive work
Depositing knowledge vs active construction
Learning through genuine participation at edges of practice
Knowledge creation through socialisation, externalisation, combination, internalisation
Sedimented disposition from repeated failure of effort
Most powerful recovery from helplessness
Celebrate effort over talent
Demonstrate through action not argument
Pathological, bureaucratic, generative
Most variation is system, not individual
Knowledge worker must define the task
Balance between exploitation and exploration
Rational systems become prisons
Beyond domination and compromise
The separation of thinking from doing
Holding environment concept; regulate the temperature
Operationalises the micro-mechanisms that maintain the double bind
Structural double bind: when signification, domination, legitimation contradict
Hidden curriculum concept extends Freire's banking education critique
Defensive routines are the organisational form of the double bind
Front/back stage is the interaction mechanism that maintains the double bind
Learned helplessness is helplessness sedimented into habitus
Hysteresis is habitus under field change — the mechanism of resistance
Loss aversion amplifies hysteresis; resistance fiercest at beginning
Mastery experience is the direct antidote to learned helplessness
Banking deposits knowledge; LPP generates knowledge through participation
Hidden curriculum is what the institutional form teaches beyond content
Radical monopoly creates the conditions for institutional iatrogenesis
Convivial institutions are the antidote to radical monopoly
Weber's iron cage is the structural precondition for Illich's radical monopoly
Taylor separates thinking from doing; Drucker reunites them
Psychological safety is the condition under which defensive routines dissolve
Front/back stage is the interaction mechanism; espoused/in-use is the knowledge structure
Convivial institutions enable exploration; manipulative ones enforce exploitation
Small wins create mastery experiences at organisational scale
Growth mindset is the disposition that enables mastery experience
Frame reflection reveals the frames behind the espoused/in-use gap
Adaptive leadership extends Follett's integration into the holding environment concept
Generative culture (Westrum) is the organisational context for psychological safety
Giddens reframes double bind as structural: signification, domination, legitimation contradicting
Blaming individuals (ignoring common cause) creates defensive routines

notes: **Central thesis**: Learning is a condition, not a process; three conditions (identity, information, institution) must be present
**Dominant thinkers**: Bourdieu (identity), Bateson (information), Illich (institution) provide the foundational mechanisms
**Key chain**: Bateson → Argyris → Edmondson traces the information condition from theory to operational practice
**Critical rivalry**: Convivial vs manipulative institutions (Illich) maps onto exploitation/exploration (March)
**Cross-condition links**: Double bind (information) connects to habitus (identity) through structuration (Giddens); hidden curriculum (institution) undermines identity condition
**Attention space**: 4–5 active positions per generation; contemporary generation dominated by Edmondson, Heifetz, Wenger, Dweck, Stacey
**Practical synthesis**: The nine probes operationalise the three conditions as diagnostic instruments for leaders
1990s: The learning organisation movement (Senge, Fifth Discipline) creates institutional demand for organisational learning programmes — the very institutionalisation Illich warned against
2020s: AI transformation programmes replicate the same pattern: process mistaken for condition, institutional learning programmes degrading the conditions they claim to create
metadata.title: Organisational Learning — Three Levers and Nine Probes

nodes.name: Pierre Bourdieu
nodes.notes: Habitus, capital, hysteresis — the mechanisms of identity reproduction and resistance to change

nodes.name: Gregory Bateson
nodes.notes: Double bind theory — contradictory messages at different logical levels with no permission to name the contradiction

nodes.name: Ivan Illich
nodes.notes: Radical critique of institutions: hidden curriculum, iatrogenesis, radical monopoly, conviviality

nodes.name: Erving Goffman
nodes.notes: Front stage/back stage, face-work, stigma, interaction order — the micro-mechanisms that maintain double binds

nodes.name: Anthony Giddens
nodes.notes: Ontological security; structuration theory — signification, domination, legitimation must move together

nodes.name: Daniel Kahneman
nodes.notes: Losses loom twice as large as gains; resistance fiercest at the beginning

nodes.name: Chris Argyris
nodes.notes: Defensive routines, Model II, espoused theory vs theory-in-use

nodes.name: Ronald Heifetz
nodes.notes: Adaptive leadership; the holding environment; distinguish technical from adaptive work

nodes.name: Paulo Freire
nodes.notes: Banking education vs problem-posing education; pedagogy of the oppressed

nodes.name: Amy Edmondson
nodes.notes: Psychological safety is the floor; safety without high standards produces comfort, not learning

nodes.name: Etienne Wenger
nodes.notes: Legitimate peripheral participation; communities of practice; learning IS participation

nodes.name: Ikujiro Nonaka
nodes.notes: SECI model; the critical step is externalisation (tacit to explicit through dialogue)

nodes.name: Peter Drucker
nodes.notes: The knowledge worker must define the task; abandonment review — would we start now?

nodes.name: Martin Seligman
nodes.notes: Learned helplessness; explanatory style — permanent, pervasive, personal predicts helplessness

nodes.name: Albert Bandura
nodes.notes: Mastery experience is the most powerful recovery path; self-efficacy

nodes.name: Carol Dweck
nodes.notes: Organisations that celebrate talent reinforce fixed mindset; those that celebrate effort reinforce growth

nodes.name: Karl Weick
nodes.notes: Small wins — demonstrate through action not argument; sensemaking

nodes.name: Donald Schön
nodes.notes: Frame reflection; most undiscussables are not facts people are hiding but frames they do not realise they hold

nodes.name: Ron Westrum
nodes.notes: Organisational typology: pathological (messengers punished), bureaucratic (messengers slowed), generative (information flows)

nodes.name: W. Edwards Deming
nodes.notes: Most variation is common cause (system); blaming individuals teaches them to hide system information

nodes.name: Tom Peters
nodes.notes: Close to the customer, close to the work; MBWA is an epistemological commitment

nodes.name: Richard Normann
nodes.notes: - The map is not just incomplete;  - it actively misleads by providing a sense of understanding that prevents seeking real understanding

nodes.name: Frederick Taylor
nodes.notes: Scientific management; the separation of thinking from doing

nodes.name: Max Weber
nodes.notes: Bureaucratic rationality; the iron cage — the system built for rational purposes becomes a prison

nodes.name: Ralph Stacey
nodes.notes: Complex responsive processes; in a convivial institution people make gestures and attend to responses

nodes.name: James March
nodes.notes: Exploitation/exploration balance; the manipulative institution is an exploitation machine

nodes.name: Nassim Nicholas Taleb
nodes.notes: Inability to abandon creates fragility; hidden liabilities shatter rather than degrade

nodes.name: Mary Parker Follett
nodes.notes: Integration over domination or compromise; joint study — parties study the situation together, not negotiate from fixed positions

nodes.name: Habitus
nodes.notes: Durable, transposable dispositions acquired through lived experience; operates below conscious awareness

nodes.name: Capital (Forms)
nodes.notes: What you are worth in the field; economic, cultural, social, symbolic; transformation threatens capital devaluation

nodes.name: Hysteresis
nodes.notes: The lag between a changed field and an unchanged habitus; the mechanism of resistance; entirely rational

nodes.name: Double Bind
nodes.notes: Contradictory messages at different logical levels with no permission to name the contradiction

nodes.name: Hidden Curriculum
nodes.notes: Lessons taught by the institutional form itself: learning requires experts; capability is certified by completion; the organisation does not trust you

nodes.name: Radical Monopoly
nodes.notes: Not a monopoly over competing products but over the conditions of the activity itself

nodes.name: Iatrogenesis
nodes.notes: When the cure causes the disease — clinical, social, and cultural levels; each deepens the next

nodes.name: Conviviality
nodes.notes: Convivial institutions provide resources people use for their own purposes; manipulative institutions prescribe, control, and credential

nodes.name: Front Stage / Back Stage
nodes.notes: Managed performance vs dropped performance; the gap between formal meetings and hallway conversations

nodes.name: Face-Work
nodes.notes: Mutual face protection; both parties benefit from the silence; interventions fail because face-work overrides training

nodes.name: Stigma
nodes.notes: - Transformation creates stigma; communicated through expressions given off;  - the stigmatised are excluded from needed conversations.

nodes.name: Ontological Security
nodes.notes: Routines provide existential continuity; disruption produces anxiety disproportionate to rational threat

nodes.name: Structuration
nodes.notes: Signification, domination, and legitimation must move together; when they don't, the organisation IS a double bind

nodes.name: Loss Aversion
nodes.notes: Losses loom twice as large as gains; resistance fiercest at the beginning

nodes.name: Defensive Routines
nodes.notes: The gap between espoused theory and theory-in-use; organisational patterns that prevent double-loop learning

nodes.name: Espoused Theory vs Theory-in-Use
nodes.notes: What people say they do vs what they actually do; the core diagnostic distinction

nodes.name: Frame Reflection
nodes.notes: Most undiscussables are not facts people are hiding but frames they do not realise they hold

nodes.name: Psychological Safety
nodes.notes: The floor for learning; safety without high standards produces comfort, not learning

nodes.name: Adaptive Leadership
nodes.notes: Distinguish technical from adaptive work; the holding environment; regulate the temperature

nodes.name: Banking Education
nodes.notes: Depositing knowledge vs problem-posing education; positions people as recipients rather than agents

nodes.name: Legitimate Peripheral Participation
nodes.notes: Newcomers learn by doing genuine tasks at the edges of practice; learning IS participation

nodes.name: SECI Model
nodes.notes: Socialisation, Externalisation, Combination, Internalisation; the critical step is externalisation through dialogue

nodes.name: Learned Helplessness
nodes.notes: Not a mood but a sedimented disposition formed through repeated experience that effort does not produce results

nodes.name: Mastery Experience
nodes.notes: The most powerful recovery path from learned helplessness; actually doing the thing and succeeding

nodes.name: Growth Mindset
nodes.notes: Organisations that celebrate talent reinforce fixed mindset; those that celebrate effort reinforce growth

nodes.name: Small Wins
nodes.notes: Demonstrate through action not argument; make learning visible before it produces measurable output

nodes.name: Organisational Typology
nodes.notes: Pathological (messengers punished), bureaucratic (messengers slowed), generative (information flows regardless of hierarchy)

nodes.name: Common Cause Variation
nodes.notes: Most variation is system; blaming individuals teaches them to hide system information

nodes.name: Knowledge Worker
nodes.notes: The knowledge worker must define the task; definition requires intimacy with the domain

nodes.name: Exploitation / Exploration
nodes.notes: The manipulative institution is an exploitation machine; exploration is structurally prevented

nodes.name: Iron Cage
nodes.notes: The system built for rational purposes becomes a prison preventing purposes from evolving

nodes.name: Integration
nodes.notes: Domination (one side wins), compromise (both give up), integration (discover a solution neither had imagined)

nodes.name: Scientific Management
nodes.notes: The separation of thinking from doing; hierarchical layers systematically remove decision-makers from reality
